description: Job has 3 flights at pickup but the quote itemizes no stairs charge. Priced fairly otherwise, so the below-market rule won't catch it — this is exactly the surprise-on-moving-day case the missing-fee rule exists for.

simulated_conversation.message: Calling for a quote on a two bedroom move, Rock Hill to Charlotte. Three flights of stairs at the pickup address.
Sure. Two bedroom local, we're looking at sixteen hundred for labor and the truck.
Does that include the three flights of stairs at pickup?
Ah — we'd have to look at that on the day. Crew'll assess it when they arrive.
So the sixteen hundred doesn't currently include a stairs charge. Anything else on top — fuel?
Fuel's a hundred flat. So seventeen hundred all in, plus whatever the stairs come to.

tool_calls.params_as_json: {"outcome": "quote_given", "company_name": "Carolina Family Moving", "base_price": 1600, "line_items": [{"label": "Labor and truck", "amount": 1600, "is_optional_or_conditional": false}, {"label": "Fuel", "amount": 100, "is_optional_or_conditional": false}], "total_price": 1700, "binding": true, "notes": "Stairs charge not quoted — to be assessed on moving day."}
